name: ONS Secure Research Service
description: The Office for National Statistics Secure Research Service (SRS) provides secure access to de-identified census, survey, and linked administrative data for accredited researchers. Researchers must hold DEA (Digital Economy Act) Accredited Researcher status to access data. Covers primarily England and Wales administrative data with some UK-wide datasets.
population_coverage: England and Wales population (census), with some UK-wide datasets
key_datasets: Annual Survey of Hours and Earnings

requirements.name: DEA Accredited Researcher status
requirements.description: Researchers must obtain Accredited Researcher status under the Digital Economy Act 2017 before applying for project access

requirements.name: Accredited project application
requirements.description: Submit a project application demonstrating public benefit and specifying data requirements

requirements.name: Output clearance
requirements.description: All outputs undergo statistical disclosure control before release from the SRS

access_steps.name: Obtain DEA Accredited Researcher status
access_steps.description: Complete the accreditation process including training and assessment under the Digital Economy Act

access_steps.name: Submit project application
access_steps.description: Apply for project approval specifying research question, datasets required, and public benefit

access_steps.name: Project review
access_steps.description: ONS reviews the project application for public benefit, feasibility, and data proportionality

access_steps.name: SRS access provisioned
access_steps.description: Approved researchers granted access to the SRS environment with specified datasets

timeline.notes: DEA Accredited Researcher accreditation (can be done in advance)
SRS workspace setup and data access provisioning

costs.notes: No fees for accredited researchers with approved projects. DEA accreditation process is also free.

strengths: Census data providing whole population coverage for England and Wales
Unique linked administrative data (education, benefits, tax, mortality)
DEA legal basis provides strong framework for data access
Long time series available for longitudinal research
limitations: Not health-specific - limited clinical data available
DEA Accredited Researcher status required (takes weeks to obtain)
Primarily England and Wales (not all datasets cover Scotland or NI)
No data extraction - all analysis within SRS
Statistical disclosure control can be restrictive for small-area analyses
recent_changes: Census 2021 microdata now available in SRS
Improved SRS platform and remote access capabilities
